name: toast
title: Toast
description: Transient notification queued imperatively with toast() and rendered in a live region. Four state variants with a per-variant icon (plus bold title) for non-color redundancy, a uniform border, pausable auto-dismiss, priority-driven politeness, an optional action button, and an accessible 44px close button. Built on Base UI Toast.
useWhen: Briefly confirming a completed async action (saved, sent, copied)
Reporting a background result that does not need a response
Acknowledging an action the user can optionally undo via an action button
avoidWhen: Critical errors the user must act on (use an inline Alert or the Field error slot so the message persists)
Anything requiring a decision or confirmation (use a Dialog)
Long or essential content a user needs time to read and re-read (auto-dismissing toasts are the wrong container)
Form field validation (use the Field error slot)

keyboard: Toasts announce via the viewport live region without any keyboard interaction: low priority is polite (aria-live=polite), high priority is assertive (rendered as an alertdialog)
F6 moves focus into the toast viewport; Tab then reaches each toast's action and close controls
The close button is a native button with an accessible name (closeLabel, default "Dismiss") and a 44px target; Enter or Space activates it
The optional action button is a native button; it does not auto-dismiss, so onAction can decide whether to close

notes: The auto-dismiss timer is pausable (WCAG 2.2.1): Base UI pauses it on hover, on focus, and while the window is blurred. The error() helper defaults to no auto-dismiss (duration 0) and high priority so users are never rushed past an error. Keep durations comfortably above reading time; use duration 0 for anything essential. Politeness is managed by Base UI from each toast's priority (low = polite, high = assertive). Positioning is fixed to the block-end/inline-end corner with logical insets, so it flips to the opposite corner in RTL; motion is slide+fade under motion-safe and fully disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
